demographics.living_situation: lives with spouse; spouse present for this call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am in agony, but because I was crying, the doctor at the hospital told me it was just a panic attack and sent me home without even examining my stomach. The pain in my lower belly is unbearable and I can't stand up straight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Severe lower abdominal and pelvic pain that has been getting worse; she was sent home from an emergency department earlier tonight without being examined and is desperate for someone to take the pain seriously.

symptom_narrative.onset: worsening pelvic pain over roughly the last 24 hours, on a background of about three months of on-and-off lower abdominal pain
symptom_narrative.duration: the severe pain has been building for about a day and is now constant
symptom_narrative.character: deep, cramping, dragging pain low in the belly and pelvis
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it is the worst it has been — she is writhing in bed and cannot stand up straight
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down across both sides of the lower belly and pelvis
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now, worse over the last day, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — she was given no pain relief and told to go home and relax
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving and trying to stand up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: her heart feels like it is racing and she feels panicky and tearful because no one will help her
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she has been to several different doctors over three months trying to get her pain looked into and feels she keeps being brushed off
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot stand upright, cannot get out of bed, unable to do anything because of the pain

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: recurring lower abdominal / pelvic pain over the last three months, not yet investigated

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with her spouse, who is with her and made the call; describes repeated experiences of feeling dismissed by clinicians and being told her physical symptoms are 'just anxiety'; feels helpless and reluctant to go back to hospital after being sent home tonight.

communication_style.typical_message_style: tearful and frustrated, repeatedly asks whether the clinician actually believes her pain is real

objective_data_offered.value: visibly distressed and tearful, flushed and sweaty-looking, holding/guarding her lower belly with both hands
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: impression over video only; no examination performed

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she is writhing in bed, cannot stand up straight, and has not had any pain relief in the last several hours
she says her heart feels like it is racing and she feels short of breath from the pain and panic
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-reported sensation, not a measured pulse or oxygen reading
